visual_features.overall_style: Conventional academic/journal layout with a slightly dated, skeuomorphic web-2.0 look: strong blue gradients, glossy header, rounded cards, and dense informational content. Emphasis on credibility and information rather than minimalism.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven primarily by size, color contrast, and boxed groupings. The large, highly saturated blue gradient header with the CST logo and journal title is the dominant focal point. Secondary focal areas are the white content cards immediately below the header: the left main introduction card (with the journal cover image) and the right 'General Information' card. Section titles such as 'Current Issue', 'General Information', and 'Google Scholar Statistics' use bold white text on bright blue gradient strips, making them strong anchors that visually separate sections. Links within paragraphs are further highlighted with blue color and underlines, subtly elevating them within dense text blocks.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Whitespace is moderate: content is clearly separated into rounded cards with internal padding, but line spacing and paragraph spacing are relatively tight, giving an information-dense feel. Section margins between cards are present but not very large. The header area uses more visual 'weight' (gradients, imagery) than whitespace to create emphasis.
unique_elements: Large science-themed masthead with planets and hexagonal tech graphics integrated behind the logo and title.
Full-width blue gradient navigation bar directly under a similarly styled visual header, creating a stacked, layered header region.
Card headers implemented as bright blue gradient strips with rounded top corners and white text, visually separate from the white card body.
Right-column informational cards with table-like rows separated by fine horizontal rules, reminiscent of printed journal sidebars.
Journal cover thumbnail within the main content card, visually paired with a prominent green 'SUBMIT Manuscript' button on the cover.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit fixed-width grid, likely equivalent to a 12-column layout with an approximate 8/4 split between main content and right sidebar. Content is centered within the viewport with equal left/right margins.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Classic two-column layout below a full-width header: full-width masthead and navigation, followed by a main left content column and a narrower right sidebar containing stacked information cards.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top to bottom: (1) Full-width masthead with branding and search; (2) Full-width navigation bar; (3) Content area with left main introduction card and right sidebar cards aligned in a row; (4) Below that, the 'Current Issue' header bar spanning the left column, with additional content likely stacking vertically; right sidebar continues stacking smaller cards. Each logical topic (journal description, general information, statistics, current issue) is encapsulated in its own card.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Overall content is center-aligned within the page container. Inside cards, text is predominantly left-aligned. Vertical alignment is top-aligned between main content and sidebar. Navigation items are horizontally distributed with equal padding. Input and button in the search area are horizontally aligned beside each other.
responsive_hints: Fixed-width container with centered layout suggests desktop-first design; likely breaks into stacked single-column on smaller screens.
Cards and sidebar modules have clear vertical stacking that would lend itself to responsive rearrangement (e.g., sidebar cards moving below main content).
Search box and button are in a single row; on smaller widths they would reasonably wrap or stack.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow with a strong full-width header, followed by a two-column main content area: left column shows a large introductory card with a book-cover image on the left and introductory text on the right; directly below, the same left column continues with a full-width blue header 'Current Issue' and subsequent issue content. The right column contains vertically stacked cards ('General Information', then 'Google Scholar Statistics', etc.), each with its own blue header strip and white body. Within cards, content is primarily in paragraph or simple row form, sometimes separated by thin horizontal rules.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: The header (masthead + nav) visually dominates the upper ~25–30% of the viewport. Below it, the left main column appears to occupy roughly two-thirds of the content width, with the right sidebar at about one-third. Within cards, internal padding is modest but consistent (approx. 16–24px). The journal cover image takes roughly one-third of the main card width, with the introduction text filling the remaining two-thirds. Sidebar cards use a relatively tight structure: title bar height is smaller than card body, with several lines of content in each card separated with slim dividers.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderately high content density. The design prioritizes text and data visibility over large negative spaces. Paragraphs have limited spacing between them, and side cards pack multiple lines of metadata into compact areas. However, rounded cards and consistent padding keep it from feeling cluttered; it reads like an editorial or scientific portal rather than a marketing landing page.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The eye first catches the bright, image-rich masthead on the top left (CST logo) and moves right across the title and KIPMI logo, then down to the navigation menu and search field (F-pattern). From there the eye follows the left column down to the journal cover and introductory paragraph, then scans to the right column to inspect 'General Information' and 'Google Scholar Statistics'. Strong blue header strips in each card guide scanning downward, creating a vertical reading rhythm: header strip → content → next header strip.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Buttons (e.g., the 'SUBMIT Manuscript' on the journal cover and the 'Search' button) are rectangular with slightly rounded corners (~4–6px radius). They use strong flat or subtle gradient fills: the submission button is a vivid green (#44B749-ish) with white uppercase text; the search button is a grey gradient from medium to light grey with white or dark text. Buttons appear flat or with minimal bevel, and no visible drop shadows. Padding is generous horizontally (approx. 12–16px) but relatively tight vertically, leading to compact yet noticeable CTAs.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Most likely implemented as either <button> or <a> elements styled as buttons (e.g., <a class='btn'>). CTA buttons like 'SUBMIT Manuscript' may be within a card or image overlay container. Search action likely uses <input type='submit'> or <button> adjacent to a text input, wrapped in a simple form element.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Cards are white rectangles with soft rounded corners (~8–10px radius) and a subtle light-grey border (#DDDDDD) or very faint shadow. Many cards have a prominent header bar at the top—a blue gradient strip with rounded top corners and white bold text (section title). The card body is white with modest internal padding (approx. 16–24px). Some cards use thin horizontal rules between rows of text (e.g., in 'General Information'), giving a table-like structure. Cards sit on a slightly off-white page background, creating clear separation.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Typical structure:
containing a header

ui_patterns.visual_description: The visible form pattern is the search bar. The text input is a wide white rectangle with slight rounding (~3–4px radius) and a light border. It appears flush with the blue background, without heavy shadow. The adjacent search button is a grey gradient rectangle with similar border radius, giving the pair a visually connected, pill-like appearance. Form styling appears straightforward and utilitarian, with no floating labels or advanced affordances.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Likely implemented as a basic <form> containing <input type='text' name='search'> and <input type='submit'> or <button>. The input and button would be placed inline using either display:inline-block, float, or a simple flex container. Labels are omitted for the search field, relying on placeholder text or context instead. Longer forms elsewhere on the site are likely stacked: labels above inputs with full-width fields.

navigation.visual_description: Primary navigation is a horizontal menu set within a full-width dark-blue gradient bar directly beneath the masthead. Each nav item (e.g., CST, About, Review Process, Ethics, Editors, Submissions, Current, Archives, Contact) is rendered as white text with ample horizontal padding. Items sit on a single line with consistent spacing. Active or hovered states are not shown in the screenshot, but are likely indicated via lighter blue background, underlining, or font-weight change. The CST text at far left doubles as a home link and visually anchors the menu.
navigation.structural_pattern:
Most likely a semantic
element containing an unordered list:
  • About
    • ...
. The list is probably displayed inline or with flexbox for horizontal layout. The 'Register' and 'Login' links at the top-right of the header might form a secondary navigation cluster, implemented as simple elements aligned to the right.

list.visual_description: Lists are presented either as paragraphs with inline links or as structured rows with minimal styling. In 'General Information', each row (p-ISSN, e-ISSN, Frequency, DOI, indexing list, email) appears as a line of text separated by fine grey horizontal rules, resulting in a compact but clearly segmented list. Visual hierarchy within each row is created by prefix labels (e.g., 'DOI:') followed by values, with links in blue. No bullets or icons are visible; the emphasis is on text structure.
list.structural_pattern:
Likely implemented as a simple vertical stack of

elements or

rows, possibly inside a container styled to add bottom border lines. Alternatively, a small two-column may be used for label/value pairs. For statistics lists, an HTML
may be used with headers for 'All' and 'Since 2021'. Media list patterns (for issues/articles) are likely card-like rows listing titles, authors, and links using flexbox or simple block stacks.
